implemented: A closed checked-in local-only alert policy maps info, warning, high, and critical conditions to report, pause/investigate, drain, and stop-ingestion/preserve-evidence actions.
Strict Draft 2020-12 schemas reject unknown alert-policy and monitoring-snapshot fields.
The collector opens the governed SQLite database read-only, verifies integrity, and reports queue, lease, reservation, retry, crash, package, dataset, and explicitly synthetic acceptance counters.
Alert evaluation is deterministic, hash-bound, severity ordered, and suppresses lower-severity duplicates in the same incident group.
Critical fixtures cover corrupt DB signals, accepted semantic defects, and source DAZ assets detected in Git.
Dashboard and daily-report documents explicitly label accepted DAZ scenes as train-only synthetic and list real-image accuracy, real-gold counts, and zero-touch operation as not claimed.
The read-only `maskfactory daz monitor snapshot` CLI emits the snapshot, alert evaluation, dashboard, and actionable daily report without launching DAZ or mutating state.

validation.regression_command: python -m pytest -q tests/test_daz_monitoring.py tests/test_daz_storage.py tests/test_daz_scheduler.py tests/test_daz_control_plane.py tests/test_daz_runtime_worker.py

alert_fixtures_proven: 99 GiB selects the high hard-floor alert and suppresses the duplicate soft-floor warning
three worker crashes produce a high drain action
retry rate at or above 20 percent produces a warning pause/investigate action
database corruption, an accepted semantic defect, or a source asset in Git produces a critical stop-ingestion/preserve-evidence action
policy evaluation is deterministic for identical captured input
unknown snapshot sections fail the closed schema
read-only collection leaves the fixture database byte-identical
remaining_acceptance: Bind production stage-duration, GPU/VRAM/CPU/RAM/IO, replay, package, coverage, dominance, near-duplicate, and useful-coverage metrics after D7 and the live worker loop provide them.
Calibrate retry and resource thresholds from the governed pilot rather than fixture defaults.
Persist and review an actual daily report from a bounded governed run, proving every alert is actionable and locally delivered.
Keep any external notification destination separately configured and prove it contains no DAZ source content before activation.
claim_boundary: This evidence proves the inactive local monitoring/alert foundation and deterministic fixtures only. It does not prove production telemetry completeness, a delivered live alert, a reviewed daily report, D7 acceptance, the production worker loop, DAZ activation, or MF-P9-12.03 completion.
